message:
fix(supply-chain): gate Mock*Verifier and MockTailscaleClient behind test-mocks feature (br-grudr)

Close the trust-bypass footgun filed in flywheel_connectors-grudr. Three
mock supply-chain verifiers in fcp-registry (MockTransparencyVerifier,
MockTufVerifier, MockSigstoreVerifier) and MockTailscaleClient in
fcp-tailscale were exported as `pub` from their respective src/lib.rs with
no `#[cfg(test)]` or feature gate. Each mock implements the same trait as
its real counterpart (TransparencyLogVerifier, TufVerifier,
SigstoreVerifier, TailscaleClient) so they were drop-in substitutes
anywhere the host expects a verifier. A single downstream
`use fcp_registry::MockSigstoreVerifier;` or
`use fcp_tailscale::MockTailscaleClient;` would yield a verifier that
accepts any bundle / a tailnet client that accepts any peer — with no
compile-time error and no runtime warning.

Implementation follows Option A from the bead (feature gate, smallest
diff). Both crates:

  - Add a Cargo feature `test-mocks = []` that is OFF by default.
  - Gate each mock type, its impl blocks, and the trait impl blocks on
    `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-mocks"))]`. Internal tests in the
    same crate still see them because `cfg(test)` is set during
    `cargo test -p <crate>`. External consumers get a compile error
    unless they explicitly opt in with `--features test-mocks`.
  - Route integration tests through `required-features = ["test-mocks"]`:
      [[test]]
      name = "no_mock_integration"
      required-features = ["test-mocks"]
    (fcp-registry pins `path = "tests/no_mock_integration.rs"` explicitly
    because the [[test]] target overrides the autodiscovery default.)

Per-file notes:

## crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs
Add `cfg(any(test, feature = "test-mocks"))` to three struct definitions,
three `impl Type { ... }` inherent blocks, and three
`#[async_trait] impl Trait for Type` blocks. Each type gets a new
`/// # Safety` doc section explicitly marking it TEST-ONLY and documenting
the feature-gate so a reader can see why it isn't visible from release
builds. Doc wording matches the bead's "belt-and-braces" suggestion.

## crates/fcp-tailscale/src/client.rs
Add `cfg(any(test, feature = "test-mocks"))` to MockTailscaleClient,
MockState (the private shared state struct), the inherent impl, and the
`impl TailscaleClient for MockTailscaleClient` block. New doc paragraph
notes the gate so future readers understand why it disappears under the
default feature set.

## crates/fcp-tailscale/src/lib.rs
Split the `pub use client::{...}` re-export so that MockTailscaleClient
is only re-exported under `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-mocks"))]`.
Non-mock types (LocalApiClient, PeerInfo, SelfNode, TailscaleClient,
TailscaleStatus) continue to be re-exported unconditionally.

## crates/fcp-registry/Cargo.toml
## crates/fcp-tailscale/Cargo.toml
Feature definition plus [[test]] target wiring. The fcp-registry Cargo.toml
comment for the feature explicitly warns that the mocks "short-circuit
supply-chain trust" and "MUST NOT be linked into a production binary" so
future readers don't accidentally flip the feature for release builds.

## Verification
`cargo build -p fcp-registry --release` and
`cargo build -p fcp-tailscale --release` MUST NOT expose Mock* types on
the public API (confirm via `cargo public-api`). `cargo test -p
fcp-registry` and `cargo test -p fcp-tailscale` continue to pass because
`cfg(test)` is set during test compilation.

## Note on unrelated `.lock().unwrap()` panics-on-poison

The bead also flagged `.lock().unwrap()` on `std::sync::Mutex` at lines
594, 605, 641, 672, 706, 720 of fcp-registry and the
`expect("system clock before year 2262")` on lines 652, 750, 942, 1035 as
secondary concerns. Those are not addressed in this commit — the
feature-gate removes the production-visibility risk, which is the P2
issue. The internal robustness cleanups stay open for a follow-up bead
that is scoped to in-mock panic surfaces rather than exposed-from-lib
trust bypasses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11,6 +11,15 @@ authors.workspace = true
 default = []
 # Enable live supply-chain verification using tough/sigstore crates
 supply-chain-live = []
+# Expose the in-memory Mock*Verifier types (MockTransparencyVerifier,
+# MockTufVerifier, MockSigstoreVerifier) through the public API.
+#
+# These verifiers short-circuit supply-chain trust by returning
+# `verified: true` for any allow-listed input. They are intended purely for
+# controlled tests and MUST NOT be linked into a production binary. The
+# feature is OFF by default so that a downstream `use fcp_registry::Mock...`
+# fails to compile unless the consumer explicitly opts in.
+test-mocks = []
 
 [dependencies]
 axum = { version = "0.8", features = ["json"] }
@@ -43,3 +52,11 @@ fcp-async-core = { path = "../fcp-async-core" }
 tempfile = "3"
 tower = { version = "0.5.3", features = ["util"] }
 uuid = { workspace = true }
+
+# Integration test that exercises the Mock*Verifier fixtures only runs when
+# the `test-mocks` feature is enabled (`cargo test -p fcp-registry --features
+# test-mocks` or `--all-features`). See the `test-mocks` feature note above.
+[[test]]
+name = "no_mock_integration"
+path = "tests/no_mock_integration.rs"
+required-features = ["test-mocks"]

@@ -11,6 +11,9 @@ authors.workspace = true
 [lints]
 workspace = true
 
+[features]
+test-mocks = []
+
 [dependencies]
 fcp-core.workspace = true
 fcp-crypto.workspace = true
@@ -28,3 +31,7 @@ tracing.workspace = true
 semver = "1.0"
 fcp-cbor = { path = "../fcp-cbor" }
 insta.workspace = true
+
+[[test]]
+name = "no_mock_integration"
+required-features = ["test-mocks"]
